calls.title: Discovery Call — Acme Corp Technical Requirements

transcript_json.text: Thanks for joining, Sarah and Mike. Today I'd love to understand your current data infrastructure and what's driving the evaluation. Can you walk me through where the pain points are?

Sure. The main driver is that we're scaling our analytics team from 5 to 20 people next quarter and our current solution just doesn't support the access control model we need. Every analyst ends up with more access than they should have.

From my side, the hard requirement is data residency. Everything needs to stay within AWS us-east-1. We had a compliance audit last quarter and this became non-negotiable. If a vendor can't confirm that, we can't move forward.

That's fully supported — we have a dedicated us-east-1 deployment option and I can loop in our solutions engineer to walk through the compliance documentation. On the access control side, can you tell me more about the model you need?

We need row-level security per team, and the ability to mask PII fields for certain roles. Budget is approved in principle — we're looking at roughly $80k annually but have flexibility if the ROI case is strong.

calls.spotlight_brief: Discovery call with Acme Corp engineering and analytics leadership. Primary drivers are access control at scale and AWS us-east-1 data residency compliance. Budget of ~$80K is approved in principle with flexibility.
calls.spotlight_key_points: 1. Hard requirement: data residency in AWS us-east-1 (compliance audit outcome). 2. Need row-level security and PII masking per role. 3. Scaling analytics team from 5 to 20 in Q1. 4. Budget ~$80K annually, approved in principle.
calls.spotlight_next_steps: 1. Alex to send compliance documentation for us-east-1 deployment. 2. Schedule technical deep-dive with solutions engineer. 3. Sarah to share current access control policy doc.
calls.spotlight_outcome: Positive — strong technical fit confirmed. Both decision makers engaged. Moving to technical validation stage.

calls.title: Technical Deep-Dive — Security & Compliance

transcript_json.text: I've reviewed the requirements Alex shared. The us-east-1 residency is straightforward — I'll walk you through our architecture. The PII masking requirement is interesting because we have two ways to implement it.

Before we get into that — we had an internal discussion and there's a new requirement. Our legal team wants an audit log of every data access event, exportable to our SIEM. This wasn't on the original list but it's now a hard requirement too.
We have audit logging built in — exportable via API and we support direct Splunk and Datadog integration for SIEM. I'll include that in the technical spec I send over.

That's actually really good news. We're on Splunk. One thing that came up — our procurement team is asking whether we can do a 2-year deal upfront for a better rate. Is that something you can structure?

calls.spotlight_brief: Technical deep-dive confirming compliance fit. New requirement added: audit log export to SIEM (Splunk). Prospect proactively asked about 2-year upfront deal for better pricing.
calls.spotlight_key_points: 1. New hard requirement: audit log exportable to SIEM — confirmed supported (Splunk integration available). 2. PII masking and RLS confirmed supported. 3. Prospect asked about 2-year upfront pricing — buying signal. 4. Us-east-1 architecture walkthrough completed.
calls.spotlight_next_steps: 1. Jordan to send full technical spec with compliance documentation. 2. Alex to prepare 1-year vs 2-year pricing comparison. 3. Next call: commercial discussion with Sarah and procurement.
calls.spotlight_outcome: Positive — all technical requirements confirmed met. Strong buying signal: prospect proactively asked about multi-year deal structure.
